_readme: All page copy, images, and buttons live here. Edit this file (then rebuild, or just save while `npm run dev` runs) to change any text, add projects or experience entries, swap images, or edit buttons and links.
profile = top-left brand (photo, name, subtitle). Nav menu links are structural and stay in index.html.
Image paths point into /public (e.g. /me.png, /images/foo.jpg). Leave an image "" to show the placeholder.
Experience items may leave body "" to show just when/role/org (no description). Projects may set link to null or { label, href }.
Buttons: 'icon' may be email | github | linkedin (drawn as an SVG icon) or omitted for a plain text button. 'href' starting with http opens in a new tab.

hero.title: Hardware that has to
hero.lede: I'm a UCLA Aerospace Engineering student graduating in 2028, looking for internships where I can design, machine, and qualify real hardware. I work across mechanical design, manufacturing, structures, and qualification testing of high performance components.

about.heading: From the bench to flight hardware.
paragraphs: I design and machine real flight hardware, taking parts from raw stock to finished and perfectly toleranced, and help qualify them through vibration and thermal-vacuum testing.

I came up through hands-on maintenance and fabrication, which is where I learned how real hardware actually fails. That instinct runs through everything I build, from NASA instrumentation and CubeSat hardware, electromechanical assemblies, and even software simulation tools.

items.title: ECLSS Sizing Tool
items.body: A Python library that sizes a crewed-mission life support system for oxygen, CO2, water balance, thermal load, and 17-species trace-contaminant buildup, with every reference value traced to NASA BVAD (TP-2015-218570). Enforces real hardware rate limits each timestep with closed-loop Sabatier and water recovery.

items.title: RP2040 Flight Data Recorder
items.body: A dual-core RP2040 flight recorder logging IMU, GPS, and barometer at 200 Hz with Mahony AHRS sensor fusion, auto-calibration, and live telemetry. Orientation validated within 2 degrees against a machinist's square. Ships with a MATLAB and Python visualizer with satellite-map overlay.

items.title: PyTherm 2D Conduction Simulator
items.body: An explicit finite-difference 2D heat-conduction solver with per-cell CFL sub-stepping and harmonic-mean interface conductances, about 100x faster than a global CFL bound. Shipped as a cross-platform PyQt6 app with a 196-material database and real-time isotherm visualization.
